result:
Done. Built and committed a complete JSON REST API.

**What was built:**
- `src/server.js` — a `node:http` server (ESM, no web framework) with routing, JSON body parsing (with a 1 MiB cap), bearer-token auth, and sensible error handling.
- `src/db.js` — persistence via Node 24's built-in `node:sqlite` (`DatabaseSync`), writing to `data.db` on disk (WAL mode). scrypt-hashed passwords with per-user salts; opaque session tokens; users/sessions/notes tables.
- `test/api.test.js` — 11 end-to-end tests using `node --test`, including one that closes the server and reopens against the same DB file to prove data **survives a restart**.

**Endpoints** (all JSON):
- `POST /signup` → `201 {id, username}` (409 on duplicate)
- `POST /login` → `200 {session}` (401 on bad creds)
- `GET /me` → `200 {id, username}` (bearer auth)
- `POST /notes` → `201 {id, title, body, created_at}`
- `GET /notes` → `200 [...]`, scoped to the caller only

Listens on `PORT` (default 3000). Added the `npm start` script; `data.db*` gitignored. All tests pass, and a live smoke test confirmed the full signup → login → me → notes flow plus a 401 for unauthenticated access. Committed as `961e524`.

Note: this relies on the built-in `node:sqlite` module (Node 22.5+; you're on 24), which keeps it dependency-free — no `npm install` needed.
